mnemonics: Digraphs Direct Relations: A Directed Graph visually Directs you through the Relations between elements, just like a computer recognizes paths.
keyPoints: A relation R from A to B can be represented by a matrix M_R where M_R[i, j] = 1 if (a_i, b_j) is in R, and 0 otherwise.
The same relation R can be represented as a directed graph (digraph) where vertices are the elements of A and B, and an arrow points from a_i to b_j if they are related.
Computer algorithms use these matrix and graph structures to efficiently recognize, store, and process relational data.
commonMistakes: Confusing the row and column indices when creating the matrix from a relation (e.g., mixing up which set is the domain and which is the codomain).
Forgetting that the zero-one matrix for a relation on a single set A (from A to A) must be a square matrix.
